title: Protein Synthesis Inhibitors
summary: مثبطات تخليق البروتين تعمل على الريبوسومات البكتيرية إما على الوحدة 30S (Tetracyclines, Aminoglycosides) أو الوحدة 50S (Macrolides, Clindamycin, Chloramphenicol, Linezolid). معظمها جراثيم-ثابتة (bacteriostatic) ما عدا الأمينوغليكوزيدات التي هي جراثيم-قاتلة (bactericidal). تنقسم المضادات الحيوية حسب نمط القتل إلى تركيز-معتمد (Concentration-dependent مثل الأمينوغليكوزيدات) ووقت-معتمد (Time-dependent مثل البنسلينات والسيفالوسبورينات والماكروليدات والكليندامايسين والليزوليد). السمية الكلوية والسمعية من أهم التأثيرات الجانبية المشتركة في هذه المجموعة.

key_drugs.name: Doxycycline
key_drugs.note: امتصاص كامل، يُفضّل في قصور الكلى، يُطرح في الصفراء

key_drugs.name: Minocycline
key_drugs.note: يعبر BBB جزئياً، يسبب دوخة وطنين

key_drugs.name: Tigecycline
key_drugs.note: IV فقط، واسع الطيف، لا يغطي Pseudomonas/Proteus/Providencia (3Ps)

key_drugs.name: Gentamicin
key_drugs.note: طيلاريميا، تآزر مع Beta-lactam في التهاب الشغاف

key_drugs.name: Neomycin
key_drugs.note: موضعي فقط أو فموي لتحضير الأمعاء، لا يُعطى حقناً

key_drugs.name: Erythromycin
key_drugs.note: 50S، بديل البنسلين في الحساسية، خطر cholestatic jaundice

key_drugs.name: Azithromycin
key_drugs.note: أطول T1/2، مرة يومياً، Chlamydia urogenital

key_drugs.name: Linezolid
key_drugs.note: 50S (23S rRNA)، يثبط MAO، neuropathy مع > 28 يوم

key_drugs.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl)
key_drugs.note: يكسر DNA، G- اللاهوائيات والطفيليات، طعم معدني

mnemonics.title: STAG-N: الأمينوغليكوزيدات
mnemonics.body:
S = Streptomycin
T = Tobramycin
A = Amikacin
G = Gentamicin
N = Neomycin
تذكّر: كأنك تقود قطيع (STAG) من الجراثيم نحو الهلاك!

mnemonics.title: Time kills the Pretty Cool Microbes, Like Crazy
mnemonics.body:
المضادات الوقت-معتمدة (Time-dependent):
P = Penicillins
C = Cephalosporins
M = Macrolides
L = Linezolid
C = Clindamycin
هذه المضادات تحتاج وقتاً لتقتل، زيادة الجرعة لا تزيد الفعالية.

mnemonics.title: Gray Baby - الطفل الرمادي والكلورامفينيكول
mnemonics.body:
تخيّل طفلاً حديث الولادة يتحول تدريجياً للون الرمادي بعد اخذ كلورامفينيكول.
السبب: الكبد لديه glucuronyl transferase ضعيف، فيتراكم الدواء ويضرب النخاع العظمي والدورة الدموية.
الصورة: طفل رمادي نائم = لا تعطِ Chloramphenicol لحديثي الولادة أبداً.

mnemonics.title: 3Ps التي لا يغطيها Tigecycline
mnemonics.body:
Tigecycline واسع الطيف جداً... لكنه يخاف من الـ 3Ps:
P = Pseudomonas aeruginosa
P = Proteus species
P = Providencia species
تذكّر: 'تايغر يصطاد كل شيء إلا الـ 3 حيوانات المحمية PPP'

mnemonics.title: ON: سمية الأمينوغليكوزيدات
mnemonics.body:
الأمينوغليكوزيدات تسبب:
O = Ototoxicity (سمية سمعية - قد تكون دائمة)
N = Nephrotoxicity (سمية كلوية - عكوسة)
تذكّر: الأمينوغليكوزيدات تطفئ (OFF) السمع والكلى!

mnemonics.title: مواقع التثبيط: Buy AT 30s, CCEL at 50s
mnemonics.body: 30S: Aminoglycosides + Tetracyclines (AT). 50S: Clindamycin, Chloramphenicol, Erythromycin, Linezolid (CCEL).

mnemonics.title: Aminoglycosides: STAG-N + Mean GNATS caNNOT kill anaerobes
mnemonics.body: الأدوية: Streptomycin, Tobramycin, Amikacin, Gentamicin, Neomycin. لا تقتل اللاهوائيات (تحتاج O2 للدخول إلى الخلية). السُّميّة (NNOT): Nephrotoxicity, Neuromuscular blockade, Ototoxicity, Teratogen.

mnemonics.title: المعتمدة على الزمن: Time kills the Pretty Cool Microbes, Like Crazy
mnemonics.body: Time-dependent killing: Penicillins, Cephalosporins, Macrolides (Pretty Cool Microbes) + Clindamycin, Linezolid (Like Crazy). يقابلها concentration-dependent مثل الأمينوغليكوزيدات والكوينولونات.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of tetracyclines?
flashcards.back: Reversibly bind the 30S ribosomal subunit and prevent tRNA from binding to the mRNA-ribosome complex, thereby inhibiting protein synthesis. Bacteriostatic.

flashcards.front: Why is Doxycycline preferred in renal failure among the tetracyclines?
flashcards.back: Because it is excreted via bile into the feces rather than through the kidneys, unlike other tetracyclines.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of tetracyclines on children and the fetus?
flashcards.back: They deposit in developing bones and teeth (bind calcium) → tooth discoloration and bone deformity. Contraindicated in children under 8 years and in pregnant women.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between aminoglycosides and tetracyclines in their 30S mechanism?
flashcards.back: Tetracyclines: reversible binding to 30S, bacteriostatic. Aminoglycosides: irreversible binding to 30S, cause misreading of mRNA and ribosome damage, bactericidal.

flashcards.front: What is the killing pattern of aminoglycosides? What is its clinical benefit?
flashcards.back: Concentration-dependent killing: greater concentration → greater killing. Combined with post-antibiotic effect, this allows once-daily dosing (less toxicity + lower cost).

flashcards.front: Why is Neomycin not given by injection?
flashcards.back: Extremely nephrotoxic when used systemically. Used only topically or orally for bowel preparation before colon surgery.

flashcards.front: What is the neuromuscular complication of aminoglycosides?
flashcards.back: Neuromuscular paralysis due to rapid concentration rise or concurrent use with neuromuscular blockers. Special risk in Myasthenia gravis. Treated with Calcium gluconate or Neostigmine.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Erythromycin and Azithromycin?
flashcards.back: Azithromycin: longer T1/2 (once-daily dosing), better against H. influenzae and Chlamydia urogenital. Erythromycin: shorter T1/2, penicillin alternative in allergy, risk of cholestatic jaundice.

flashcards.front: What is the serious cardiac side effect associated with macrolides?
flashcards.back: QT interval prolongation, which increases the risk of cardiac arrhythmias.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of clindamycin? What are its main uses?
flashcards.back: Binds the 50S subunit and inhibits translation (bacteriostatic). Used against: Gram-positive organisms, anaerobes (Bacteroides fragilis), and bone infections (good bone penetration).

flashcards.front: What is the C. difficile risk associated with clindamycin?
flashcards.back: Clindamycin is among the antibiotics with the highest risk of causing C. difficile infection (CDI/pseudomembranous colitis) and carries a Boxed Warning.

flashcards.front: What is Gray Baby Syndrome? Why does it occur?
flashcards.back: Accumulation of Chloramphenicol in neonates due to deficient glucuronyl transferase in the liver → bone marrow suppression and cyanosis → 'gray' appearance. Can be fatal.

flashcards.front: What is the main serious warning for Linezolid with prolonged use?
flashcards.back: Irreversible peripheral neuropathy and optic neuritis (→ blindness) with use >28 days. Also inhibits MAO → serotonin syndrome.

flashcards.front: What is Demeclocycline and its distinctive uses?
flashcards.back: Demeclocycline is a tetracycline used to treat SIADH (syndrome of inappropriate ADH secretion) because it inhibits the action of ADH on the kidneys.

flashcards.front: What is Metronidazole and its mechanism of action?
flashcards.back: Metronidazole (Flagyl) is a nitroimidazole that cleaves bacterial and parasitic DNA. Effective against Gram-negative anaerobes and parasites (Giardia, Trichomonas, Entamoeba). Main side effect: metallic taste.

flashcards.front: Why are aminoglycosides not used for meningitis?
flashcards.back: Because they do not cross the blood-brain barrier (BBB) at adequate concentrations even in the presence of meningeal inflammation.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive use of Streptomycin?
flashcards.back: Tuberculosis, Plague, and Brucellosis; given intramuscularly (IM).

flashcards.front: What foods should be avoided with tetracyclines?
flashcards.back: Milk, cheese, and anything containing Ca++, Mg++, Al+++, or Iron — they form non-absorbable chelates with tetracyclines.

flashcards.front: What is the difference among macrolides and how are they excreted?
flashcards.back: Macrolides are excreted hepatically, not renally. They inhibit CYP450, causing drug interactions. Azithromycin has the longest T1/2.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Linezolid and how does it differ from other 50S inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Linezolid binds to 23S rRNA in the 50S subunit and inhibits formation of the 70S initiation complex. This binding site is unique and differs from that of Chloramphenicol and macrolides.

flashcards.front: Which protein synthesis inhibitors act on 30S?
flashcards.back: Aminoglycosides and tetracyclines.

flashcards.front: Which inhibitors act on 50S?
flashcards.back: Macrolides, Clindamycin, Chloramphenicol, Linezolid.

flashcards.front: Why are aminoglycosides bactericidal and require O2?
flashcards.back: They require aerobic transport to enter the cell → inactive against anaerobes; concentration-dependent killing.

flashcards.front: What are the toxicities of aminoglycosides?
flashcards.back: Nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity/vestibular toxicity, neuromuscular blockade, teratogenicity (STAG-N → NNOT).

flashcards.front: What is the toxicity of Chloramphenicol?
flashcards.back: Aplastic anemia and Gray Baby Syndrome.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive toxicity of tetracyclines?
flashcards.back: Tooth discoloration and inhibition of bone growth; contraindicated in pregnancy and children.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of macrolides (Erythromycin)?
flashcards.back: Bind 50S and inhibit translocation; bacteriostatic.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of Linezolid?
flashcards.back: Active against MRSA and VRE; risk of myelosuppression and serotonin syndrome.

mcqs.q: A 5-year-old child needs treatment for a Chlamydia infection. Which antibiotic is contraindicated?
mcqs.explanation: Tetracycline is contraindicated in children under 8 years of age due to its deposition in developing bones and teeth, causing discoloration and deformities.

mcqs.q: Which tetracycline is preferred in patients with renal failure?
mcqs.explanation: Doxycycline is excreted via bile (feces) rather than through the kidneys, making it the only safe tetracycline in renal failure.

mcqs.q: A patient receiving an aminoglycoside develops progressive muscle paralysis. What is the correct treatment?
mcqs.explanation: Calcium gluconate or Neostigmine reverse the neuromuscular blockade caused by aminoglycosides.

mcqs.q: Which aminoglycoside is used orally for bowel preparation before surgery?
mcqs.explanation: Neomycin is highly nephrotoxic systemically, so it is given orally (not absorbed) or topically only.

mcqs.q: A pregnant patient has Chlamydia infection. What is the most appropriate antibiotic?
mcqs.explanation: Erythromycin is the optimal choice for Chlamydia in pregnancy. Tetracyclines are contraindicated in pregnancy due to their effects on fetal bones and teeth.

mcqs.q: Which of the following drugs causes Gray Baby Syndrome?
mcqs.explanation: Chloramphenicol accumulates in neonates due to deficient glucuronyl transferase → Gray Baby Syndrome with bone marrow suppression and cyanosis.

mcqs.q: A patient has been taking Linezolid for 6 weeks. What is the most concerning complication?
mcqs.explanation: Linezolid with use > 28 days causes irreversible peripheral neuropathy and optic neuritis that may lead to blindness.

mcqs.q: What are the 3Ps not covered by Tigecycline?
mcqs.explanation: Tigecycline does not cover the 3Ps: Pseudomonas, Proteus, Providencia despite its very broad spectrum.

mcqs.q: What is the main difference in killing pattern between aminoglycosides and penicillins?
options: Aminoglycosides are time-dependent, penicillins are concentration-dependent
Aminoglycosides are concentration-dependent with PAE, penicillins are time-dependent without PAE
mcqs.explanation: Aminoglycosides: concentration-dependent with post-antibiotic effect (PAE), allowing once-daily dosing. Penicillins: time-dependent with virtually no PAE.

mcqs.q: Which macrolide is first-line for urogenital Chlamydia infection?
mcqs.explanation: Azithromycin is first-line for urogenital Chlamydia due to its very long T1/2 and a single sufficient treatment dose.

mcqs.q: Aminoglycosides do not work against anaerobes because they:
Gray Baby Syndrome is associated with:

high_yield.point: Aminoglycosides قاتلة، معتمدة على التركيز (concentration-dependent)، تحتاج O2 للدخول → لا تعمل على اللاهوائيات.
سُمّية Aminoglycosides: كلوية + سمعية/دهليزية + حصار عصبي عضلي + ماسخة للجنين.
Chloramphenicol → فقر دم لا تنسجي + متلازمة الطفل الرمادي (Grey baby).
معظم مثبطات تخليق البروتين موقفة للنمو (bacteriostatic) ما عدا الأمينوغليكوزيدات (قاتلة).
